title: UPI OC No 205 FY 24 25 – Introduction of Auto Top up on UPI LITE
text:
Nell

evar arene Pret

NATIONAL ae CORPORATION OF INDIA

NPCI/UPI/OC No. 205/2024-25 27" August 2024

To,
All UPI Members

Dear Sir/Madam,

Subject: Introduction of Auto Top-up on UPI LITE

This is in reference to NPCI circular NPCI/UPI/OC No. 138/2021-22, dated 16 March 2022 and
NPCI/UPI/OC No. 169/2023-24, dated 31** August 2023, whereby guidelines to operationalize
UPI LITE small value transactions have been introduced. UP! LITE enhances the user experience
by allowing PIN less transactions under 2500 with a maximum UPI LITE balance limit of 2000 at
any point of time.

To further augment the UP! LITE user experience, Auto Top-up on UP! LITE is being introduced.
With this new feature, the UPI LITE balance will automatically be reloaded by an amount chosen
by the user, not exceeding UPI LITE balance limit, whenever the balance falls below a minimum
limit set by them. This increases the convenience and adaptability of UPI LITE by automating the
process of reloading funds. Additionally, users shall be provided with the option of revoking the
Auto Top-up mandate at any point of time by the PSP/App.

Since the balance resides with user’s bank and the UPI LITE can be deleted anytime with instant
refund of UPI LITE balance to user's main bank account, the requirement of pre-debit notification

(PDN), which is a usual mechanism for merchant mandate payments, has been dispensed with.
Members (Banks & PSP/App) are required to ensure below guidelines are followed

1. Issuer banks live on UPI LITE shall support the functionality of Auto Top-up on UPI LITE
wherein the banks should allow creation of UP! LITE mandate and allow debits whenever
the request comes from the PSP/App.

2. UPI Apps shall support the necessary functionality and interface on their App for the
customer to use Auto Top-up on UPI LITE.

3. Members shall ensure that the mandate created successfully are executed successfully.
All the necessary validation should be present at the time of mandate creation.

4, PSP/Apps shall put necessary controls to limit the number of auto-replenishment
transactions to 5 per day per LITE accouni.

1001A, The Capital, B Wing, 10" Floor,
Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra (E), Mumbai 400 051.

T: +91 22 40009100 F; +91 22 40009101 www.npci.org.in
CIN: U74990MH2008NPL189067

Nell

er openers Prva
NATIONAL mao 7 COM ORM DNOF INDIA

5. Issuer bank shall ensure the necessary communication to the customer for all the mandate

related operations.

In addition to this bank shall also be required to follow the necessary guidelines released by NPCI
on Auto Top-up on UPI LITE from time to time. Members who are live on UPI LITE are requested
to hereby note the changes and enable the feature by 31* October 2024.

Yours Sincerely,
\ 9

Kurial Kalawatia

Chief of Products
